source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division — Postcards Collection
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1928)
title: Ingersoll Hotel Bldg., Ketchikan, Alaska
description: Historic gelatin silver postcard of the Ingersoll Hotel at the corner of Mission Street and Mill Street in Ketchikan. Thwaites moved his photography and curio shop into the Ingersoll in 1924. Produced by the Thwaites Studio, card number 5668, between 1900 and 1940.
alt: Historic sepia postcard of the three-story Ingersoll Hotel building in downtown Ketchikan with period automobile parked outside
verifiedBySession: 2026-04-12 — downloaded from loc.gov and visually confirmed
